citations.description: Ticket comment by RebarBotNava: 'AC4 PyPI: curl pypi.org/pypi/nava-rebar/json -> info.version == 0.10.1' and 'release.yml run 30065382823: authorize/build-once/wheel_test/sdist_test/publish/github_release/mcp_registry all success. pypi deployment approval granted by JoeOakhartNava'

findings.criterion: 0.10.1 published to PyPI: 'curl -s https://pypi.org/pypi/nava-rebar/json | python3 -c "import json,sys;print(json.load(sys.stdin)[\"info\"][\"version\"])"' prints 0.10.1.
findings.detail: This criterion is untagged and therefore classified codebase-verifiable. PyPI publication is an external operational fact with no codebase evidence available. The ticket comment attests 'curl pypi.org/pypi/nava-rebar/json -> info.version == 0.10.1' with run ID 30065382823, but a codebase-verifiable criterion cannot be satisfied by a ticket comment alone. No repository file or symbol confirms the live PyPI state.
findings.remediation: Tag this criterion '[operator-attested]' in the ticket, then record the concrete curl output (showing version=0.10.1) as a ticket comment with the timestamp and approver name — which the existing comment already does. Alternatively, the ticket author should re-tag AC4 as operator-attested so the existing attestation (run 30065382823, JoeOakhartNava approval) can be credited.

citations.description: Ticket comment by RebarBotNava: 'AC5 GitHub Release v0.10.1: target=cffbe72 (=origin/main HEAD), draft=false prerelease=false, /releases/latest -> v0.10.1'

findings.criterion: v0.10.1 tag + GitHub Release created at the released main SHA: 'gh release view v0.10.1 --json tagName,targetCommitish,isLatest' shows the tag, marked Latest.
findings.detail: This criterion is untagged and therefore classified codebase-verifiable. GitHub Release creation is an external operational fact with no codebase evidence available. The ticket comment attests 'GitHub Release v0.10.1: target=cffbe72, draft=false prerelease=false, /releases/latest -> v0.10.1' but a codebase-verifiable criterion cannot be satisfied by a ticket comment alone. No repository file confirms the GitHub Release exists.
findings.remediation: Tag this criterion '[operator-attested]' in the ticket, then the existing attestation (target=cffbe72, draft=false, prerelease=false, isLatest=true, run 30065382823) already provides the required specifics and would satisfy the criterion.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
